title: Tax Season Volume Surge — 45% Increase Predicted, 6 Days Out
description: Historical 3-year pattern predicts 45% call volume increase starting April 10. Peak impact April 12–17 across general banking and mortgage servicing queues. Current staffing plan covers only 28% of the projected increase — a 22-agent gap at peak hours. 6 days until impact.

confidence.rationale: Three-year tax-season pattern is highly stable (±4% YoY). Coverage-gap math is deterministic from authoritative WFM schedule.

title: New Hire Ramp Falling Behind — AHT 14:30 vs. 9:00 Target at Week 6
description: Current training cohort (12 agents, started March 1) is averaging 14:30 AHT vs. 9:00 target at the 6-week mark. Historical cohorts reached 9:00 by week 5. Knowledge base adherence is 62% vs. 85% benchmark — agents skip the guided resolution flow 38% of the time, going directly to supervisor escalation. Cohort may need 3 additional weeks before handling peak volume.

confidence.rationale: AHT and KB-adherence are direct telemetry; the 'needs 3 more weeks' projection is anchored to 8 prior cohort ramps with ±5-day variance.

title: Overtime Budget at 78% — Projected to Hit 140% by Quarter End
description: Q2 overtime spend is at 78% of budget with 10 weeks remaining. Current trajectory projects 140% of budget by quarter end — a $26,000 overage. Top driver: unplanned PTO coverage in mortgage servicing. Hybrid scheduling approach (8 OT shifts + cross-trained agents) can stay within 96% of budget while meeting tax season demand.

confidence.rationale: MTD spend is authoritative; 140% projection assumes current burn-rate continues. Hybrid-scheduling 96% target has ±5 pp variance.
